title: Vitis humulifolia
taxonomy: Vitis humulifolia is classified as a species of grapevine in the genus Vitis, which belongs to the family Vitaceae. This family consists of about 14 genera and over 1,000 species of vines and shrubs. The genus Vitis itself comprises over 60 species, many of which are native to Asia and are used for wine production. Vitis humulifolia is native to China and is a deciduous shrub or small tree. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other members of the Vitaceae family.
uses: Vitis humulifolia, a species of grapevine native to East Asia, is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fever. In traditional Chinese medicine, Vitis humulifolia is believed to have properties that help lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential anti-diabetic and anti-cancer properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and benefits of Vitis humulifolia.

constituents: Vitis humulifolia, a species of grapevine native to China, contains various bioactive compounds including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. These active constituents are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties and have been studied for their potential health benefits. parts: Vitis humulifolia, a type of grapevine native to China, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. intro: Vitis humulifolia, also known as Chinese grapevine, is a species of flowering plant in the grape family native to China and Korea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, offering various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help protect against cardiovascular disease, cancer, and other chronic conditions. The bioactive constituents of Vitis humulifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Vitis humulifolia is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to create formulations for treating various health conditions.
